questions.text: What does 'Al-Adiyat' mean?
questions.answer: The [Steeds] that run / The Coursers.

questions.text: How are the steeds described in the first verse?
questions.answer: They run panting (Dab-ha).

questions.text: What do the steeds produce with their hooves?
questions.answer: Sparks of fire (Al-Muriyat).

questions.text: When do these steeds strike their enemies?
questions.answer: In the morning (Al-Mughirat).

questions.text: What do the steeds stir up in their wake?
questions.answer: Dust (Naq'a).

questions.text: How do the steeds arrive at the enemy?
questions.answer: In the center / middle of the assembly (Wasat-na).

questions.text: What is the statement Allah makes after these oaths?
questions.answer: Indeed, mankind is ungrateful (Kanud) to his Lord.

questions.text: Is the human being a witness to his own ungratefulness?
questions.answer: Yes, 'And indeed, he is to that a witness' (Verse 7).

questions.text: What is man 'intense' in the love of (Verse 8)?
questions.answer: Wealth / Good things (Al-Khayr).

questions.text: What question does Allah ask regarding the graves?
questions.answer: 'Does he not know that when the contents of the graves are scattered/overturned...?'

questions.text: What will be 'obtained' or 'brought out' from the breasts (hearts)?
questions.answer: What is within the breasts (intentions/secrets).

questions.text: How is Allah described at the end of the Surah regarding His knowledge?
questions.answer: As 'Al-Khabir' (All-Acquainted/All-Aware).

questions.text: What is 'Kanud'?
questions.answer: A person who is ungrateful, focusing only on calamities and forgetting blessings.

questions.text: What is the significance of the horses in the opening verses?
questions.answer: They represent power, speed, and loyalty to their masters, contrasting with the human's lack of loyalty to Allah.

questions.text: What is 'Al-Khayr' refer to in Verse 8?
questions.answer: Worldly wealth and possessions.

questions.text: What is the 'scattering' of the graves?
questions.answer: The resurrection of the bodies.

questions.text: Why will the secrets of the hearts be brought out?
questions.answer: Because judgment is based on the reality of a person's faith and intention, not just outward show.

questions.text: Does Allah know the secrets of the hearts only on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: No, He is always aware, but on that Day, the awareness will be manifested to everyone.

questions.text: What is the 'Morning Strike' (Sub-ha) signifying?
questions.answer: The suddenness and clarity of the coming judgment or military victory.

questions.text: What is the main warning of Surah Al-Adiyat?
questions.answer: A warning against materialism and ungratefulness, reminding us of the final accountability of the heart.
